title: Make it Monday Forum – Anchorage Assembly Annual Report
description: Monthly 'Make it Monday' business forum presented by the Anchorage Chamber of Commerce, featuring the Anchorage Assembly Annual Report.

title: First Friday Art Walk
description: Monthly First Friday art walk in which downtown Anchorage galleries celebrate new works by local artists, with receptions, hors d'oeuvres, and opportunities to meet the artists.

title: Yonder Mountain String Band
location: Atwood Concert Hall, Alaska Center for the Performing Arts, Anchorage
description: Live bluegrass/jam band performance by Yonder Mountain String Band at the Atwood Concert Hall.

title: New Grass Roots
location: Alaska Center for the Performing Arts, Anchorage
description: Live music event at the Alaska Center for the Performing Arts, presented via CenterTix.

title: Anchorage Salmon Run
description: Annual 10K, 5K, and 2K fun run supporting Anchorage-area school PTAs. A community running event for all ages and abilities.

title: Seward Sprint Triathlon
description: Annual sprint triathlon held in Seward on the Kenai Peninsula. Open to individuals and relay teams.

title: Spenard Farmers Market – Opening Day
location: 26th Ave & Spenard Road (under the windmill), Anchorage
description: Opening day of the Spenard Farmers Market, a community-run Saturday market featuring locally grown produce, handcrafted goods, prepared foods, and live music, running weekly from May through September.

title: Cheney Lake Park Spring Cleanup
description: Community volunteer cleanup at Cheney Lake Park as part of the Anchorage Citywide Spring Clean-Up, organized by Anchorage Park Foundation.

title: Community Fair at Chugiak Eagle River Senior Center
location: Chugiak Eagle River Senior Center, Eagle River
description: Annual community fair at the Chugiak Eagle River Senior Center featuring local vendors, activities, and community gathering.

title: Steps for Strong Minds 5K Run/Walk
description: A family-focused mental health awareness 5K run and walk event in Anchorage. Supported by Skinny Raven Sports.

title: Spring Duathlon Series – Race 1
description: First race in Anchorage's Spring Duathlon Series featuring supersprint and youth duathlon distances, with a virtual option available.

title: Kachemak Bay Shorebird Festival
location: Alaska Maritime National Wildlife Refuge Visitor Center, Homer
description: The 34th annual Kachemak Bay Shorebird Festival is Alaska's largest wildlife festival, offering nearly 200 events including guided birding trips, expert talks, kayak tours, hands-on workshops, art exhibitions, and activities for all ages during the peak shorebird migration.

title: Love a Nurse Run 5K
description: A 5K run and walk benefiting the Foundation for Alaska Nurses, celebrating National Nurses Week.

title: Girls on the Run 5K
description: Annual Girls on the Run celebratory 5K focused on building confidence and accomplishment for youth participants.

title: Annual Matson Community Cleanup – Anchorage Running Club
description: The Anchorage Running Club hosts its annual community trail cleanup behind the Peanut Farm, open to all volunteers.

title: Kinderlauf Family Fun Run
description: A family-friendly 5K and 2K fun run with a dedicated kids' race component, designed for all ages and abilities.

title: Roald Dahl's Matilda the Musical – Anchorage Community Theatre
location: Sydney Laurence Theater, Alaska Center for the Performing Arts, Anchorage
description: Anchorage Community Theatre presents its 70th season closing production of Roald Dahl's Matilda the Musical, featuring high-energy dance numbers and catchy songs. Shows run May 9–19, Wed–Fri at 7pm, Sat at 2pm & 7pm, Sun at 3pm.

title: Jo Koy – Stand-Up Comedy
description: Stand-up comedy performance by comedian Jo Koy at the Alaska Airlines Center. Event is 21+ unless accompanied by a parent or guardian.

title: Bike to Work Week – Anchorage
location: Anchorage trail and road network, Anchorage
description: National Bike to Work Week (May 11–17) culminating in Anchorage Bike to Work Day on May 15. Commuters are encouraged to log bike commutes for prizes, with treat stations along popular cycling routes.

title: Make it Monday Forum – America250: Alaska's Week of Dreams
description: Monthly 'Make it Monday' business forum celebrating America's 250th anniversary with a focus on Alaska's Week of Dreams programming.

title: Spring Duathlon Series – Race 2
description: Second race in Anchorage's Spring Duathlon Series featuring supersprint and youth duathlon distances.

title: Anchorage Bike to Work Day
description: Anchorage's annual Bike to Work Day celebration with morning and afternoon commute windows, treat stations along bike routes, and volunteer trail counters. Part of National Bike to Work Week (May 11–17).

title: Chiodos – Rock Concert
description: Live outdoor rock concert featuring post-hardcore band Chiodos at Williwaw Social's outdoor venue.

title: Rock Emo Legends
description: Live rock and emo music show at Williwaw featuring multiple artists from the rock/emo genre.

title: Law Enforcement Torch Run – Soldotna
description: Annual 5K run benefiting Special Olympics Alaska, carried by law enforcement and community participants on the Kenai Peninsula.

title: Law Enforcement Torch Run – Anchorage
description: Annual 5K run benefiting Special Olympics Alaska, with law enforcement and community runners carrying the Flame of Hope through Anchorage.

title: Exit Glacier Race – 5K & 10K
location: Resurrection Roadhouse, Herman Leirer Road, Seward
description: Annual 5K and 10K road race hosted by Seward Parks & Recreation, starting at the Resurrection Roadhouse near Exit Glacier with an out-and-back route through stunning Kenai Peninsula scenery.

title: Anchorage Market & Festival – Opening Weekend
description: Opening weekend of the Anchorage Market & Festival, Alaska's largest open-air weekend market with over 300 vendors offering artisan crafts, Alaska-grown produce, diverse food vendors, and live entertainment every Saturday and Sunday through mid-September.

title: Janie Fricke & Moore & Moore – Country Concert
location: William A. Egan Civic & Convention Center, Anchorage
description: Country music concert featuring veteran country singer Janie Fricke alongside country duo Moore & Moore at the Egan Convention Center.

title: Gold Nugget Triathlon
location: Bartlett High School, 1101 Golden Bear Drive, Anchorage
description: Annual all-women sprint triathlon dedicated to improving the lives of women and girls through athletics, with 1,650 in-person participants plus a virtual option. USAT sanctioned.

title: Shoreline Mafia – Hip Hop Concert
description: Live hip-hop concert featuring Shoreline Mafia at Williwaw in downtown Anchorage.

title: Alaska Arbor Day Tree Planting
description: Celebrate Alaska Arbor Day by volunteering to plant trees across Anchorage neighborhoods, organized by the Anchorage Park Foundation.

title: Alaska Arbor Day Community Events
description: Alaska Arbor Day volunteer event to plant trees across Anchorage in partnership with the Anchorage Park Foundation.

title: SOJA – Reggae Concert
description: Live reggae concert by SOJA (Soldiers of Jah Army) at Moose's Tooth Pub & Pizzeria in Anchorage.

title: Cousin Curtiss – Live at Palmer Alehouse
description: Live country/Americana music performance by Cousin Curtiss at the Palmer Alehouse.

title: Gunsight Mountain Music Festival 2026
location: Gunsight Mountain Lodge, 123 N Glenn Hwy, Chickaloon
description: Three-day outdoor music festival at Gunsight Mountain Lodge in Chickaloon featuring Confederate Railroad and other country acts. Camping available on site.

title: Cousin Curtiss – Live at Creekbend Cafe
description: Live country/Americana performance by Cousin Curtiss at Creekbend Cafe in the Mat-Su Valley.

title: Trent Waldron Half Marathon & 10K
location: Westchester Lagoon / Margaret Sullivan Park, Anchorage
description: Annual half marathon and 10K hosted by the Anchorage Running Club, starting and finishing at Westchester Lagoon with a scenic out-and-back course along Chester Creek and University Trails. Honors Marcie Trent and Larry Waldron.

title: 3 Rings Circus – Family Show
description: Family circus event at Sullivan Arena featuring acrobatics, clowns, and circus acts over two days, May 23–24.

title: Sawyer Brown & Montgomery Gentry – Country Concert
location: Alaska Airlines Center, 3550 Providence Drive, Anchorage
description: Double-bill country concert featuring Sawyer Brown and Montgomery Gentry at the Alaska Airlines Center.

title: Purity Ring – Electronic Music Concert
location: Wendy Williamson Auditorium, 2533 Providence Drive, Anchorage
description: Electronic pop duo Purity Ring performs live at the Wendy Williamson Auditorium on the UAA campus.

title: Chase Matthew – Country Concert
location: George M. Sullivan Sports Arena, 1600 Gambell Street, Anchorage
description: Country artist Chase Matthew performs live at the Sullivan Arena in Anchorage.

title: Skinny Raven Twilight 12K & Skinny Mini 6K
location: 8th & H Street (start) / Rose Garden, Delaney Park Strip (finish), Anchorage
description: Popular Friday evening 12K and 6K urban road race with live bands along the route, closing the A/C Street bridge for a Government Hill loop, then through Downtown, the Coastal Trail, and Westchester Lagoon, finishing at the Rose Garden with Bear Tooth food and Broken Tooth beverages.

title: Golden Girls: The Laughs Continue
location: Atwood Concert Hall, 621 W 6th Avenue, Anchorage
description: Touring comedy stage show based on the classic Golden Girls TV series, with multiple performances May 30–31 at the Atwood Concert Hall.

title: Anchorage Tacos and Tequila Fest
location: Sullivan Arena Parking Lot, 1600 Gambell Street, Anchorage
description: 21+ outdoor festival featuring multiple taco vendors, tequila tastings, Lucha Libre wrestling, a Chihuahua beauty pageant, and a concert headlined by Bone Thugs-N-Harmony, Mark McGrath of Sugar Ray, Sugar Hill Gang, Furious 5, and DJ Skribble.
